author: gb9t
content:
Strategy locked in by user (2026-05-09):

- Source: shallow clone of torvalds/linux mainline → ~/linux-src
- Baseline version: latest mainline (commit hash to be pinned in 00-baseline.md once clone finishes)
- Compat target: FULL ABI — syscall numbers + /proc & /sys layouts + kernel-module ABI. Existing distros must boot unchanged. Maximum scope.
- Starting point: top-down — overview/glossary/conventions docs first, then walk the kernel directory tree

Document layout (decision):
- Use .design/<area>/<subarea>/<slug>.md nested directories. Flat naming will not scale to thousands of docs. Skill writes a single file per invocation; we override the path convention deliberately and document it in 00-overview.md.

Phase plan:
- Phase A (foundations): 00-overview.md, 00-glossary.md, 00-rust-conventions.md, 00-baseline.md, 00-index.md
- Phase B (subsystem overviews): one 00-overview per top-level kernel/ directory (arch, mm, fs, net, kernel, drivers, ipc, security, crypto, block, lib, sound, virt)
- Phase C (component-level designs): drill into each subsystem (e.g. kernel/sched/cfs.md)
- Phase D (syscall ABI specs): per-syscall semantic specs for the ~400-syscall surface
- Phase E (driver subsystems): drivers/* — likely the largest single area

Out of scope for this session: any code, any decisions about Rust crate layout in the implementation tree (that belongs to the implementing instance). This session writes specs only.
